question_text:
Balanced Binary Tree

Given a binary tree, determine if it is height-balanced (depth of two subtrees of every node never differs by more than 1).
explanation: Recursively calculate the height of subtrees. If any subtree is unbalanced, return -1. A tree is balanced if heights of left and right subtrees differ by at most 1.
answer:
def isBalanced(root):
    def check(node):
        if not node: return 0
        left = check(node.left)
        if left == -1: return -1
        right = check(node.right)
        if right == -1: return -1
        if abs(left - right) > 1: return -1
        return max(left, right) + 1
    return check(root) != -1
